title: Document Phase 1: Config module and YAML file

handoff_summary.completed: Added config.py to Project Layout section. Added config.yaml to .orchestrator/ directory listing. Added new Configuration section documenting dataclasses, key functions, and YAML structure.
handoff_summary.remaining: Phase 2 beads that wire config into runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, and prompts.py will need corresponding CLAUDE.md updates once those modules import from config.py.
handoff_summary.next_action: No further action needed for this bead.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Phase 2 beads that modify runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, or prompts.py will need to import from config.py and should depend on this bead.

conflict_risks: Phase 2 beads that modify runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, or prompts.py will need to import from config.py and should depend on this bead.

execution_history.summary: Updated CLAUDE.md to document the Phase 1 config module: added config.py to Project Layout, added config.yaml to .orchestrator/ listing, and added a new Configuration section describing the three dataclasses (OrchestratorConfig, SchedulerConfig, BackendConfig), public functions (load_config, default_config), and YAML file structure.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
